sections.paragraph:
αὐγάζω 1 aor. inf. αὐγάσαι (s. αὐγή) relates to brightness or clarity in various senses

① see (so in poets Soph. et al.; Lycophron v. 941 αὐγάζων φάος=seeing the light; Philod.: Anth. Pal. 5, 123, 3 and Philo, Mos. 2, 139; cp. Nägeli 25f) τὶ someth. Metaph., of the gospel’s light 2 Cor 4:4 (s. φωτισμός 1). This is the most likely interpr. (see e.g. Ltzm., Windisch, H-DWendland, NRSV).

② intr. shine forth (PGM 3, 143; 4, 1636; 2558; Lev 13:24ff; 14:56; GrBar 9:8) suggested by some (for example, Sickenberger) for 2 Cor 4:4 (s. 1) can hardly do without αὐτοῖς, which is actually added in many mss.—DELG s.v. αὐγή. M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Lycophron Lycophron , trag., IV–III b.c.—List 5

Philod Philod , I b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

someth. someth. = something

interpr. interpr. = interpreted, interpretation

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Ltzm. Ltzm. = HLietzmann, commentator and editor of various works

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

NRSV NRSV = New Revised Standard Version of the NT—List 6

intr. intr. = intransitive

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

ff ff = (and) following

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 149
